limitations: The clean clone used the same Mac and could reuse content-addressed Python and R package caches; dependency identities remained locked by uv.lock and renv.lock.
Dependency restoration used network access. The P006 closure does not establish production egress denial or host isolation.

The local clean clone skipped the Linux-only R worker integration test. The same exact commit separately passed all 1,247 tests, worker parity, scientific handoff, SBOM, dependency, secret, and container gates in GitHub-hosted Ubuntu CI.

The GitHub-hosted runner image is named but is not represented by an immutable runner-image digest; source, actions, base image, Python, uv, R, packages, and the built container image are separately pinned or recorded.

This closure is limited to the named synthetic whole-text fixtures and fixed worker boundary. It does not establish raw-text preprocessing, literary-corpus validity, benchmark accuracy, stability calibration, public workflow behavior, FAIR run export, Pinocchio findings, or production deployment.

notes: Exact audit commit d676d90 was fetched from the GitHub origin into a fresh remote no-hardlinks clone, checked out detached, restored from committed Python and R lockfiles, and passed the complete local repository gate with 1,246 tests, one declared Linux-only skip, 100% measured statement and branch coverage, all frozen evidence validators, metadata, 84 provenance records, repository scanning, R parsing, and the R lock gate. The post-run clone remained versionably clean. The same exact commit passed GitHub-hosted Ubuntu CI run 29350106890 with all 1,247 tests, the fixed worker parity and scientific handoff validators, 100% measured coverage, SBOM/dependency/secret gates, and canonical Linux amd64 container construction. This run satisfies P006-AC-08 only within the declared fixture-local worker boundary; P007-P015 claims remain locked.
